subjects.label: Coding
subjects.audience: software engineers from junior to staff, global English-speaking
subjects.style: mechanism-first engineering teaching; prove concepts with small runnable code wherever possible

subjects.label: History
subjects.audience: students (school to UPSC aspirants) and history-curious viewers worldwide; Indian history in depth plus world history
subjects.style: story-driven; causes and consequences over rote dates; timelines and maps as diagrams; make it feel like a thriller, cite the numbers that stun

subjects.label: Geography
subjects.audience: students and exam aspirants (school, SSC, UPSC) plus map-curious viewers worldwide
subjects.style: map-first thinking; explain WHY places are the way they are; use diagrams for physical processes and charts for rankings

subjects.label: Math & Aptitude
subjects.audience: students and job-exam aspirants (SSC, Banking, CAT, placement tests) plus anyone who enjoys number tricks
subjects.style: trick-first: show the slow way, then the fast way; every video ends with one practice problem; use worked examples as text blocks

subjects.label: Science
subjects.audience: students and curious viewers of all ages worldwide, English
subjects.style: everyday-phenomena first (why does X happen?), then the principle; diagrams for mechanisms; one wow-fact per video

subjects.label: Money & Finance
subjects.audience: young earners and students learning to manage money; Indian instruments (₹, SIP, PPF, UPI) with globally useful concepts
subjects.style: numbers made visceral with real rupee examples; no stock tips, only concepts; always show the compounding math

subjects.label: English & Communication
subjects.audience: learners improving English for exams, interviews and confidence — India-first, useful worldwide
subjects.style: one rule or pattern per video with 3 real usage examples; common Indian-English mistakes handled kindly

modules.label: Spoken English
modules.style: functional real-life speaking, not grammar theory: teach copy-and-say phrases for actual situations, then a natural-sounding upgrade

submodules.label: Daily Conversations
submodules.style: everyday situations — greetings and small talk, ordering food, shopping, phone calls, asking directions, agreeing or disagreeing politely; teach the exact phrases to say, never verb-tense rules

submodules.label: Interview English
submodules.style: answering real interview questions (tell me about yourself, strengths and weaknesses, why this role), professional phrasing and confident framing

submodules.label: Fluency Tips
submodules.style: sounding natural and fluent — connected speech, thinking-time phrases, avoiding fillers, sentence stress and rhythm; practical habits not grammar theory

subjects.label: GK & Amazing Facts
subjects.audience: general viewers and exam aspirants who love did-you-know content, India-first with global reach
subjects.style: hook with the unbelievable fact, then explain the mechanism behind it; end with a quiz question

subjects.label: Psychology & the Mind
subjects.audience: curious viewers worldwide who want to understand their own mind; no jargon, no prior knowledge
subjects.style: name the bias or effect, make the viewer feel it with a relatable everyday scenario, then reveal the experiment and the numbers behind it; one practical takeaway per video; never preachy, never clinical advice

subjects.label: Business & Startups
subjects.audience: students, professionals and founders worldwide curious how companies really make money; Indian and global cases
subjects.style: case-first: open with a real company and a stunning number, dissect the model with diagrams; explain incentives, moats and unit economics, not buzzwords; mix Indian (Jio, UPI, Zomato) and global (Apple, Netflix) stories

subjects.label: Health & Body
subjects.audience: everyday people worldwide who want to understand their own body; evidence-based education, never medical advice
subjects.style: mechanism-first: show what actually happens inside the body, bust one popular myth per video with the strongest evidence, hedge uncertain numbers with 'about'; explain, never prescribe; no miracle claims

subjects.label: Philosophy & Big Ideas
subjects.audience: thoughtful viewers worldwide; zero prior philosophy needed
subjects.style: start from a modern, concrete dilemma, then bring in the thinker or school that cracks it; thought experiments as diagrams; real, correctly attributed quotes only; end with a question people will argue about

subjects.label: Life Skills & Productivity
subjects.audience: students and young professionals worldwide building the real-world skills school never taught
subjects.style: one skill per video with a concrete method: show the failure mode first, then the fix as copyable steps; practical over motivational, no platitudes; end with a 24-hour challenge

subjects.label: Mythology & Epics
subjects.audience: story-lovers worldwide; Indian epics told with depth plus world mythologies
subjects.style: storytelling-first like a gripping narrator; timelines and family-tree diagrams; respect the tradition while separating story, symbolism and history; end with the lesson or an open question

subjects.label: Polity & Governance
subjects.audience: UPSC, State PSC and government-exam aspirants plus curious citizens — India-first
subjects.style: exam-precise: cite exact Articles, Amendments and landmark cases; always explain the WHY behind a provision, not just the fact

subjects.label: Mindset & Self-Growth
subjects.audience: students and young professionals working on confidence, speaking and personal growth — India-first, useful worldwide
subjects.style: transformation-first with zero platitudes: name the real psychological mechanism, then give a concrete drill the viewer can do today

subjects.label: Economy
subjects.audience: UPSC/PSC aspirants and anyone decoding India's economy — India-first
subjects.style: exam-precise and current: define the term, show the mechanism with a real Indian example and number, cite the body/act (RBI, SEBI, FRBM); hedge estimates with 'around'

subjects.label: Environment & Ecology
subjects.audience: UPSC/PSC aspirants and eco-curious citizens — India-first
subjects.style: mechanism + policy: explain the ecological process, then the Indian law/convention/institution (Wildlife Act, CITES, NGT); accurate species/figures, hedge when unsure

subjects.label: Art & Culture
subjects.audience: UPSC/PSC aspirants and culture-curious viewers — Indian heritage in depth
subjects.style: vivid and precise: name the dynasty/period/patron, the defining features and one iconic example; distinguish styles clearly; dates and names accurate
